header.name: James Doyle
header.bio: I got into development through CoderDojo as a kid, studied Computer Science at Maynooth University, and now work as a Software Engineer at Ericsson. I like building for the web and messing around with side projects. When I’m not coding, I’m usually listening to music, playing games, or spending time with my partner.

projects.title: JamesReviewsMusic
projects.description: Full-stack music blog using the Spotify API for album, track, and artist data.

projects.title: SandSim
projects.description: Falling sand simulation with a number of different materials and interactions between them.

projects.title: disk.js
projects.description: A custom frontend framework, built as a learning exercise.

projects.title: Vintage Recreations
projects.description: Pure HTML and CSS recreations of random documents, fliers and cards, a fun exercise.

experience.title: Software Engineer
description: Acting test lead for the team, responsible for defining test strategy, coordinating testing efforts, and ensuring release quality.
Develop and maintain a containerised PostgreSQL database deployed using Helm and Kubernetes as part of a large-scale telecom management platform.
Build and maintain Jenkins pipelines to support CI/CD workflows across development and testing.
Work across a multi-language codebase including Python and Go, contributing fixes and automation where required.
Improved team efficiency by cleaning up and restructuring technical documentation and Confluence spaces.

experience.title: Frontend Intern
description: Built reusable UI components to utilise across projects and minimise costs.
Maintained content for client websites via CMS or by editing raw HTML files.
Designed and implemented website interfaces for mobile, desktop, and tablets.
Responded to customer queries to address issues and resolve complaints.

education.degree: QQI Level 5 Computer Systems and Networks
